title: Buffer Overflow, Underflow, and Copy Boundaries · Memory Trace: Off By One Proof
summary: A network frame rewriter moves overlapping regions and an off-by-one defect writes before or beyond the packet buffer. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem off by one proof increment in bounded frame transformation. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Derive half-open ranges and prove start, count, end, and terminator relationships for changed empty and maximum cases.
Use generation counters, handles, quarantine, or delayed reclamation where stale asynchronous references cannot be excluded.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Off By One Proof · Theory Off By One Proof
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory native-systems team handles memory case 8732. Produce bounded C evidence to move overlapping bytes inside proven bounds; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Derive half-open ranges and prove start, count, end, and terminator relationships for changed empty and maximum cases.

steps.title: Build Off By One Proof evidence · Theory Off By One Proof
steps.instruction:
For this theory demonstration of cmem-off-by-one-proof, write the complete compile-ready C program headed "/* Evidence: cmem-off-by-one-proof-8rqlc0 */" for changed case 8649. It must move overlapping bytes inside proven bounds, reject one changed boundary, print an observable result and invariant, and return nonzero when the invariant fails. Keep instant code within the PicoC 3.2.2 practice subset; do not claim C23, optimizing compiler, sanitizer, ABI, thread, operating-system, FFI, load, or production evidence until its explicit controlled transfer gate passes.
